steps.title: Convert the percent decrease
steps.content: A $4.2\%$ decrease per year means the population is multiplied by $1-0.042=0.958$ each year. The starting population in 2010 is $7.15$, so the exponential model starts with $7.15$ and uses multiplier $0.958$.

steps.title: Apply the exponential decay formula
steps.content: From 2010 to 2020 is $10$ years, so apply the multiplier $10$ times: $$7.15(0.958)^{10}.$$ This matches choice D.
